---
title: "a04"
author: "Denis Cruz"
date: "2026-03-26"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
# Intro: in this assignment we practice join and wrangling using bird survey data.
```


```{r}
help("lubridate")
```

```{r}
#loading packages 
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(here)
library(lubridate)
```

```{r} 
# loading data
bird_observations <- read_csv(here::here("assignments/data/raw/52_pp52_birds_1.csv"))
surveys <- read_csv(here::here("assignments/data/raw/52_pp52_surveys_1.csv"))
sites <- read_csv(here::here("assignments/data/raw/52_pp52_sites_1.csv"))
taxalist <- read_csv(here::here("assignments/data/raw/52_pp52_taxalist_1.csv"))
```

```{r} 
# question 2, looking at data
dim(bird_observations)
head(bird_observations)
glimpse(bird_observations)
summary(bird_observations)
names(bird_observations)
unique(bird_observations$species_id)
```

```{r}
#question 3 create subset
birds_subset <- bird_observations %>% 
  filter(species_id %in% c("BHCO", "RWBL")) %>% 
  filter(site_id %in% c("LI-W", "NU-C"))
```

```{r}
# merge and join practice
birds_left <- birds_subset %>% 
  left_join(y = sites, by = "site_id")

birds_inner <- birds_subset %>% 
  inner_join(y = taxalist, by = "species_id")

birds_inner_left <- birds_subset %>% 
  left_join(y = taxalist, by = "species_id")

bird_observations <- bird_observations %>% 
  rename(bird_obs_notes = notes)

bird_obs_subset <- bird_observations %>% 
  full_join(y = taxalist, by = "species_id") %>% 
  full_join(y = sites, by = "site_id") %>% 
  full_join(y = surveys, by = c("site_id", "survey_id")) %>% 
  select(survey_date, common_name, park_name, bird_count, observer)
```

```{r}
unique(bird_obs_subset$observer)
#7 unique names

bird_obs_subset <- bird_obs_subset %>% 
  mutate(
    observer = if_else(
      condition = observer == "Josh Burns",
      true = "J. Burns",
      false = observer
    )
  )

unique(bird_obs_subset$observer)
```
```{r}
# ao4 Assignment start 
#question 1

bird_obs_subset <- bird_obs_subset %>% 
  mutate(
    survey_month = month(survey_date),
    survey_month = factor(survey_month)
  )
```

```{r}
# question 2

bird_obs_subset <- bird_obs_subset %>% 
  mutate(
    survey_month = month(survey_date),
    survey_month = factor(survey_month)
  ) %>% 
  relocate(survey_month, .after = survey_date)
```

```{r}
#question 3
bird_obs_subset_filtered <- bird_obs_subset %>% 
  filter(park_name %in% c("Lindo", "Orme", "Palomino", "Sonrisa"))
```

```{r}
# question 4
birds_by_park_month <- bird_obs_subset_filtered %>% 
  group_by(park_name, survey_month) %>% 
  summarize(total_birds = sum(bird_count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

birds_by_park_month
```

```{r}
## Summary: yes I think the data we created is informative, it is easier to see as it reduced many individual observations into a simpler table. Maybe to imprve the data we can check for values more carfeully which would help decided what joins or merges are better 

```
